items.front: Basic radar services for VFR aircraft per 7-6-1
items.back: Three components: 1. Safety alerts. 2. Traffic advisories. 3. Limited radar vectoring (on pilot request only).

items.front: Prerequisite for positioning in approach sequence per 7-6-4
items.back: Identify the aircraft BEFORE taking any action to position it. You cannot sequence what you cannot identify.

items.front: VFR holding for approach sequencing per 7-6-5
items.back: Hold VFR aircraft over the initial reporting fix or a fix near the airport when holding is required to establish an approach sequence.
Do NOT assign landing sequence numbers unless responsibility delegated in LOA or facility directive. Approach control sequences, tower numbers.
1. Inform tower of aircraft position. 2. THEN instruct pilot to contact tower. Aircraft may also be instructed to contact tower on initial contact when procedure established.
Provide: wind direction and velocity, favored runway, altimeter setting, traffic advisories, pertinent airport conditions. Coordinate with the overlying approach control or ARTCC.
